meta.title: Compliance Framework Checklists
meta.description: Structured checklists and requirement summaries for major compliance frameworks relevant to software companies, SaaS, fintech, and healthcare technology.
meta.disclaimer: This dataset is for informational purposes only. Consult qualified legal and compliance professionals before making compliance decisions.

key_controls.requirement: Logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures have been implemented to support access restriction to information assets
New internal and external users are registered, and authorized credentials are issued; users are removed or modified upon role change or termination
Role-based access control and principle of least privilege enforced
Detection and monitoring tools identify anomalies indicating malicious acts, natural disasters, or errors affecting ability to meet objectives
Security incidents are identified and classified; response and recovery activities are performed
Changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures are authorized, documented, tested, and approved
Risk assessments are performed; residual risk is identified; risk mitigation activities are implemented
Current processing capacity and usage are maintained; future needs are forecasted and managed
Environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure are in place

frameworks.name: PCI DSS v4.0
frameworks.full_name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

merchant_levels.level_1: Over 6 million Visa/Mastercard transactions per year; QSA audit required
merchant_levels.level_2: 1 to 6 million transactions per year; self-assessment questionnaire
merchant_levels.level_3: 20,000 to 1 million e-commerce transactions per year
merchant_levels.level_4: Under 20,000 e-commerce or under 1 million other transactions

requirements.title: Install and maintain network security controls
Apply secure configurations to all system components
Protect cardholder data with strong cryptography during transmission over open networks
Protect all systems and networks from malicious software
Develop and maintain secure systems and software
Restrict access to system components and cardholder data by business need to know
Identify users and authenticate access to system components
Restrict physical access to cardholder data
Log and monitor all access to system components and cardholder data
Test security of systems and networks regularly
Support information security with organizational policies and programs

frameworks.name: GDPR
frameworks.region: EU / EEA (and any org processing EU resident data)
applicable_to: Any organization processing EU/EEA resident personal data
frameworks.max_fine: 4% of global annual turnover or EUR 20 million (whichever is higher)

key_requirements.requirement: Lawful basis for processing (consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, legitimate interests)
Privacy notice: inform subjects what data you collect, why, how long, who you share with, their rights
Right to erasure (right to be forgotten): honor deletion requests within 30 days
Right to data portability: provide data in machine-readable format on request
Data protection by design and by default
Data Processing Agreement (DPA) required with all processors
Appropriate technical and organizational security measures
Notify supervisory authority of breach within 72 hours
Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for high-risk processing
Appoint Data Protection Officer (DPO) if processing at scale or sensitive categories

frameworks.name: HIPAA
frameworks.full_name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
frameworks.issuing_body: US Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)

key_requirements.requirement: Limit uses and disclosures of PHI to minimum necessary; provide Notice of Privacy Practices
Honor patient rights: access, amendment, accounting of disclosures, restrictions
Conduct and document risk analysis; implement risk management plan
Implement access controls: unique user IDs, emergency access procedure, automatic logoff, encryption
Audit controls: hardware, software, and procedural mechanisms to record and examine access
Transmission security: encrypt ePHI in transit; guard against unauthorized access during transmission
Business Associate Agreements (BAA) required with all vendors handling PHI
Notify HHS and affected individuals within 60 days of discovery of breach affecting 500+ individuals

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022
applicable_to: Any organization seeking internationally recognized information security certification
frameworks.audit_frequency: Annual surveillance audits; full recertification every 3 years

key_clauses.title: Context of the organization: stakeholders, scope, ISMS
Leadership: policy, roles, top management commitment
Planning: risk assessment and treatment, security objectives
Support: resources, competence, awareness, communication, documentation
Operation: risk assessment execution, risk treatment implementation
Performance evaluation: monitoring, internal audit, management review
Improvement: nonconformity, corrective action, continual improvement

frameworks.name: CCPA / CPRA
frameworks.full_name: California Consumer Privacy Act / California Privacy Rights Act
frameworks.region: California (and any org collecting data of CA residents above thresholds)
frameworks.applicable_to: For-profit businesses that: collect CA resident personal info AND (revenue > $25M OR buy/sell data of 100,000+ consumers OR derive 50%+ revenue from selling personal info)
key_consumer_rights: Right to know what personal information is collected
Right to opt-out of sale or sharing of personal information
Right to non-discrimination for exercising CCPA rights
Right to correct inaccurate personal information (CPRA)
Right to limit use of sensitive personal information (CPRA)
key_requirements: Privacy policy must disclose categories collected, purpose, retention period, third parties
Provide Do Not Sell / Do Not Share opt-out mechanism
Honor deletion requests within 45 days (one 45-day extension allowed)
Data Processing Agreements with service providers
Annual privacy audits for high-risk processing (CPRA)

frameworks.name: SOX
applicable_to: Foreign private issuers listed on US exchanges

key_sections.title: Corporate Responsibility for Financial Reports: CEO/CFO certify accuracy of financial statements
Management Assessment of Internal Controls: annual report on effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting (ICFR)
Real time disclosure: disclose material changes rapidly
Criminal penalties for altering documents or destroying records
Corporate responsibility for financial reports: criminal penalties for CEO/CFO false certifications

it_general_controls: Access management and segregation of duties

frameworks.name: FCA Regulatory Framework
applicable_to: Crypto asset businesses registered with FCA

key_regulations.title: Senior Management Arrangements, Systems and Controls: governance, risk management, compliance
Principles for Business: 12 principles including Principle 12 (Consumer Duty)
Conduct of Business Sourcebook: client communications, suitability, best execution
Client Assets Sourcebook: safeguarding client money and assets
Principle 12: deliver good outcomes for retail customers; assess and evidence outcomes annually
Payment Services Regulations: SCA, open banking API obligations under PSD2/PSR
Money Laundering Regulations: customer due diligence (CDD), enhanced due diligence (EDD), SAR filing

frameworks.application_process: FCA authorisation application; typically 6 to 12 months; fees from GBP 1,500 to 25,000+
frameworks.capital_requirements: Depends on firm type; Payment Institution min EUR 20,000 to 125,000
